skill.name: lark-calendar
skill.description: Lark Calendar integration providing comprehensive calendar and event management capabilities including viewing, creating, and managing events, attendees, and availability.
skill.summary: Manage your Lark calendar with AI assistance - schedule meetings, check availability, and coordinate with team members seamlessly.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The scanned files are Markdown documentation (.md) containing CLI command examples, not executable code. The 'external_commands' detections are bash examples in documentation showing lark-cli usage. The 'path traversal' patterns are markdown relative links. The 'high entropy' and 'weak crypto' findings are spurious detections on text documentation with Chinese characters. No security risks identified.

content.user_title: Manage your Lark Calendar with AI assistance
content.value_statement: Scheduling meetings and coordinating calendars is time-consuming. This skill automates calendar management, finds optimal meeting times, and handles event creation through natural language commands.
actual_capabilities: View and search calendar events with customizable date ranges
Create new events with attendees, descriptions, and reminders
Check user availability and free/busy time slots
Get intelligent meeting time recommendations for multiple participants
Manage event attendees including add and remove operations
Handle recurring events with standard recurrence rules
limitations: Requires lark-cli tool to be installed and configured with valid Lark credentials
Cannot book meeting rooms directly - room IDs must be provided if needed
FreeBusy queries only work with the user's primary calendar, not subscribed calendars
Bot accounts must explicitly specify a user-id when querying availability

use_cases.title: Quick meeting scheduling for busy professionals
use_cases.description: Schedule meetings by describing your needs in natural language. The skill finds available time slots, checks participant availability, and creates the event with all details.
use_cases.target_user: Business professionals and managers who frequently coordinate meetings

use_cases.title: Team availability coordination
use_cases.description: Find optimal meeting times across multiple team members by querying their free/busy status and getting intelligent time recommendations that work for everyone.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads and project coordinators managing group schedules

use_cases.title: Personal calendar management
use_cases.description: View daily agendas, search past events, and manage personal schedules through conversational commands without navigating complex calendar interfaces.
use_cases.target_user: Individual contributors seeking streamlined calendar workflows

prompt_templates.title: View today's schedule
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me my calendar for today

prompt_templates.title: Schedule a meeting with specific time
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a meeting titled 'Project Review' tomorrow at 2 PM with Alice and Bob for 1 hour

prompt_templates.title: Find available meeting times
prompt_templates.prompt: Find a good time this week for a 45-minute meeting with the engineering team

prompt_templates.title: Complex scheduling with constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: Schedule a product demo with the client team next week. Need 90 minutes, exclude lunch hours (12-1pm), and make sure all department heads can attend

output_examples.output:
Today's Schedule (March 11):

9:00 AM - 9:30 AM  Daily Standup
10:00 AM - 11:00 AM  Product Review Meeting
2:00 PM - 3:00 PM   1:1 with Alice

You have 3 events scheduled today.

output_examples.input: Find time for a 30-minute meeting with Alice this afternoon
output_examples.output:
Available time slots for you and Alice:

1. 2:00 PM - 2:30 PM - Both participants are free
2. 3:30 PM - 4:00 PM - Both participants are free
3. 4:30 PM - 5:00 PM - Both participants are free

Which time works best for you?

best_practices: Always verify the meeting time and attendees before confirming event creation
Use the freebusy command to check availability before proposing meeting times to others
Include clear agenda and description in meeting invites to help attendees prepare
anti_patterns: Do not create meetings in the past - the system will reject these requests
Avoid scheduling without checking participant availability first
Do not include sensitive information in event titles that may be visible in notifications

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need the lark-cli tool installed and configured with your Lark workspace credentials. The skill will guide you through authentication if not already set up.

faq.question: Can this skill schedule meetings with external participants?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add external participants using their email addresses. However, availability checking only works for internal Lark users.

faq.question: How does the skill handle recurring meetings?
faq.answer: The skill supports standard recurrence rules (RFC 5545). You can create daily, weekly, monthly, or custom recurring events by specifying the recurrence pattern.

faq.question: Can I modify or delete existing events?
faq.answer: Yes, you can update event details like time, title, and attendees. You can also delete events you own. The skill will confirm before making changes.

faq.question: Does this work with shared calendars?
faq.answer: Yes, you can view and manage events on shared calendars you have access to. Use the calendar-id parameter to specify which calendar to operate on.

faq.question: What happens if a meeting room is unavailable?
faq.answer: The skill does not automatically book rooms. If you need a meeting room, provide the room ID (omm_ prefix) and it will be added to the event. Room availability is not automatically checked.
